next_steps: Understand why discord.js doesn't keep event loop alive in systemd context
Investigate controller's 15-second gateway lifecycle (possible startup health timeout)
Compare openclaw behavior with --verbose vs without (bonjour starts in verbose, not in plain)
Consider starting from scratch: deploy one VM with known-good config and iterate
All services stopped and disabled - need to re-enable when ready

current_status.all_vms: openclaw-gateway STOPPED and DISABLED on all 8 VMs
current_status.zeus_config_state: openclaw.json has discord token hardcoded (from bak_token_inject), groupPolicy:open set

root_cause_confirmed.original_crash_loop: Doctor wizard set channels.discord.enabled=false on all VMs -> no Discord WS -> event loop drained -> clean exit (code 0) -> Restart=always -> loop
root_cause_confirmed.fix_applied: Set channels.discord.enabled=true on all 8 VMs - this fixed 7 VMs but Zeus still cycles
root_cause_confirmed.zeus_specific_issue: Internal 15-second cycle in the openclaw controller - gateway subprocess spawns, connects Discord WS (confirmed: TCP to 162.159.136.232:443, 2777 bytes RecvQ), then controller kills/restarts gateway after ~15s
zeus_additional_issues: groupPolicy:allowlist set by doctor wizard (fixed to 'open')
Token hardcoded in JSON (matches Mac Mini pattern)

key_discoveries.bonjour_difference: When running 'openclaw gateway --verbose' manually, bonjour/mDNS starts and keeps event loop alive. When running via systemd (without --verbose), bonjour does NOT start in app logs. This may be the difference.
key_discoveries.discord_connection_confirmed: TCP connection to 162.159.136.232:443 IS established from gateway subprocess. Discord sends 2777 bytes (RecvQ). But gateway subprocess exits 5-10s after Discord responds.
key_discoveries.controller_15s_cycle: The openclaw controller (parent process) restarts the gateway child every ~15 seconds. systemd only counts outer restarts (NRestarts stays low). The inner cycle is openclaw's own health/startup logic.
key_discoveries.mac_mini_reference: jeff-mac-mini runs OpenClaw 2026.2.15 (older), VMs run 2026.2.26. Mac Mini has discord token hardcoded in JSON, groupPolicy:open, dmPolicy:pairing, historyLimit:30, allowFrom:[Jeff_user_ID]
key_discoveries.all_vms_high_cpu: All VMs show 128-145% CPU on openclaw-gateway process - this appears constant, may be normal for OpenClaw or may indicate tight startup loop on all VMs

hypothesis_for_next_session.primary: The controller kills the gateway subprocess after ~15s if Discord doesn't become 'ready' (authenticated + guild data received). Discord.js needs GUILD_MEMBERS and MESSAGE_CONTENT privileged intents enabled in Discord Developer Portal, or the bot needs to be in at least one server to receive GUILD_CREATE events and complete the 'ready' sequence.
hypothesis_for_next_session.secondary: OpenClaw 2026.2.26 may have changed behavior where it requires Discord to fully authenticate before considering the gateway 'healthy'. Without guilds/intents the READY event never fires.
hypothesis_for_next_session.tertiary: Something about PrivateTmp=true or the systemd environment prevents bonjour from starting, which was the only other keepalive mechanism

things_to_check_next: Check Zeus Discord bot in Discord Developer Portal: are privileged intents (Guild Members, Message Content) enabled?
Check if any Discord bot (Zeus/Athena/etc) is invited to any Discord servers - if not, READY event never fires
Check openclaw --help or docs for 'startup timeout' or 'channel connect timeout' config option
Compare openclaw 2026.2.26 vs 2026.2.15 changelog for gateway behavior changes
Try running openclaw with DEBUG=* or NODE_DEBUG=* to see discord.js internal logs
Try: openclaw channels add --channel discord to interactively set up the channel properly
Consider rolling back to openclaw 2026.2.15 on one VM to confirm version is the issue
